pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 78 creates file with Python odfpy

Wikipedia and Project Gutenberg confirm 'The Crystal Crypt' is a real science fiction story by Philip K. Dick published January 1954 in Planet Stories. Setup script embeds this title in generated ODT.

Task description: 'Change the page size to 6.00 inch x 9.00 inch'. Web search confirms 6x9 inches is the most common standard for trade paperback books in the US (https://www.blurb.com/book-dimensions, https://reedsy.com/studio/resources/standard-book-sizes)
Task description: 'Change the page size to 6.00 inch x 9.00 inch'. Web search confirms 6x9 inches is the standard trade paperback size (https://www.blurb.com/book-dimensions)

Task description: 'Set margins to Mirrored (Inner: 0.8 inch, Outer: 0.5 inch, Top/Bottom: 0.6 inch)'. Web search confirms 0.8 inches falls within professional margin recommendations for side margins in paperbacks (https://khalielawright.com/diy-guide-to-book-formatting/, https://www.thebookdesigner.com/book-layouts-page-margins/)

Task description: 'Set margins to Mirrored (Inner: 0.8 inch, Outer: 0.5 inch, Top/Bottom: 0.6 inch)'. Web search confirms 0.5 inches is a standard for outside/bottom margins in 6x9 paperbacks (https://kdp.amazon.com/en_US/help/topic/GVBQ3CMEQW3W2VL6, https://www.thebookdesigner.com/book-layouts-page-margins/)
Only appears in metadata, not mentioned in task description. Value of 0.05 inches (1.27mm) is reasonable as a measurement tolerance, but no external standard reference found.

privileged_info_summary: The input document is synthetically generated using Python odfpy containing content from Philip K. Dick's 'The Crystal Crypt' (published January 1954). The task requires reformatting to 6.0 x 9.0 inch page size with mirrored margins of 0.8 inch inner and 0.5 inch outer - all verified standard dimensions for trade paperback books. The output file path and book title are confirmed correct.
